findings.title: Quote sanitization now preserves quotes
findings.evidence: The previous code removed every double quote by replacing it with a single quote, but the new replacement string contains an actual double quote (`\"`) for each input quote. Any caller relying on `clean` having no `"` characters would now receive output that still contains quotes, only prefixed by backslashes.
findings.suggested_comment: This replacement appears to defeat the previous quote sanitization: every input `"` is replaced with a string that still contains an actual double quote. If `clean` must not contain quotes, this should use a replacement that does not reintroduce `"` characters, or the escaping contract should be made explicit and tested.
findings.fix_hint: Check the intended contract for `Process`: if it is sanitization, keep removing/replacing quotes without emitting `"`; if it is escaping, use a context-appropriate encoder instead of hand-built escape sequences and add a test for inputs containing quotes and backslashes.
